name: Herrera del Duque Castle Loop Trail
name_original: Herrera del Duque — circular Puebla de Alcocer
description: This 8.8-mile mountain bike route in La Siberia offers a moderate ride through dehesa landscapes and past traditional farmhouses.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Herrera Del Duque offers diverse terrain within the UNESCO Biosphere Reserve of La Siberia Extremeña. The region features rolling mountains, expansive pastureland, and significant bodies of water like the García de Sola Reservoir and the Guadiana River. Mediterranean forests with cork oaks and holm oaks provide varied backdrops, while the Golondrinos and Navas mountain ranges offer challenging ascents and descents. This landscape creates a rich environment for mountain bike trails of varying difficulties. Best mountain bike trails around Herrera Del Duque The most popular mountain bike trail is Embalse de García Sola y Casas de Don Pedro — circular en BTT por Puebla de Alcocer, a 21.0 miles (33.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 18 minutes to complete. This route explores the scenic surroundings of the García de Sola Reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Herrera del Duque Castle Loop Trail, a moderate 8.8 miles (14.1 km) path. It offers views of the historic Herrera del Duque Castle and the surrounding landscape. Local mountain bikers also love the View of Mountainous Landscape loop from Fuenlabrada de los Montes, a 42.9 miles (69.1 km) trail leading through mountainous landscapes, often completed in about 6 hours 38 minutes. Mountain biking around Herrera Del Duque is defined by large reservoirs, Mediterranean forests, and rolling mountain ranges. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 80 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Herrera Del Duque's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Herrera Del Duque?
A: There are over 20 mountain bike trails available around Herrera Del Duque on komoot, offering a range of experiences from moderate to difficult.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Herrera Del Duque?
A: The terrain around Herrera Del Duque is diverse, featuring expansive pastureland, rolling mountains, and significant bodies of water like the García de Sola Reservoir. You'll encounter Mediterranean forests with cork oaks and holm oaks, as well as challenging ascents and descents in the Golondrinos and Navas mountain ranges. Trails vary from mostly paved surfaces to unpaved and difficult segments.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Herrera Del Duque?
A: Yes, there is at least one easy mountain bike trail suitable for families. While many routes are moderate to difficult, you can find options that cater to varying fitness levels. Look for trails with less elevation gain and smoother paths for a more relaxed family outing.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see while mountain biking?
A: The region, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, offers stunning natural beauty. You can explore the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334082" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;García de Sola Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, ride along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3559367" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiana Riverside Trail&lt;/a&gt;, and pass through Mediterranean forests. The historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1201103126/herrera-del-duque-castle-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Herrera del Duque Castle&lt;/a&gt; is also a prominent landmark offering commanding views.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1201072706/embalse-de-garcia-sola-y-casas-de-don-pedro-circular-en-btt-por-puebla-de-alcocer" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de García Sola y Casas de Don Pedro — circular en BTT por Puebla de Alcocer&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular route exploring the García de Sola Reservoir. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21931823" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Mountainous Landscape loop from Fuenlabrada de los Montes&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Herrera Del Duque?
A: Spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for mountain biking in Herrera Del Duque, offering milder temperatures and lush landscapes. Summers can be very hot, while winters are cooler but still offer good riding days. The region's diverse flora and fauna are particularly vibrant in spring.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the region is known for its strategic lookouts. You can find panoramic vistas at points like Consolación Pass Lookout, Navas Pass, Castle Pass, and La Berrea lookout. These offer breathtaking views of the Guadiana River, surrounding villages, and opportunities for wildlife observation.

Q: What is the difficulty range of mountain bike trails in this area?
A: The mountain bike trails around Herrera Del Duque cater to various skill levels. You'll find routes ranging from easy, suitable for any fitness level, to moderate paths, and more challenging routes classified as difficult, which may require good to very good fitness and advanced riding skills, sometimes even pushing your bike.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails?
A: While many natural areas in Spain are dog-friendly, it's always best to check specific trail regulations or local signage regarding dogs, especially within the UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. Generally, dogs are welcome on trails if kept under control, but some protected areas might have restrictions.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the Cíjara Nature Reserve?
A: Yes, you can explore the Cíjara Nature Reserve by mountain bike. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38159027" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cíjara Nature Reserve loop from Fuenlabrada de los Montes&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging route that takes you through this well-preserved natural area, known for its diverse flora and fauna.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Herrera Del Duque?
A: The komoot community frequently praises the diverse landscapes, from large reservoirs and Mediterranean forests to rolling mountain ranges. Riders appreciate the variety of trails suitable for different ability levels and the stunning natural beauty of the UNESCO Biosphere Reserve of La Siberia Extremeña.
tips: Located to the south of Castilblanco, this town is surrounded by ferruginous springs. In it we find a castle from the Almohad period, devoid of walls and decoration and built in an octagonal plan on a high rock to the south of the town, on a difficult-to-reach peak.

The Herrera del Duque Castle, located in the province of Badajoz (Extremadura), is an impressive 15th-century fortress that dominates the landscape of the La Siberia region from the top of the Sierra Chamorro.
It is said that wailing sounds can be heard at night from the "Tower of Sighs," and there are legends about a hidden treasure protected by a curse.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Herrera del Duque - circular Puebla de Alcocer mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: This is considered a moderate mountain bike ride. It requires a good fitness level but is generally without significant technical complications, making it suitable for riders who prefer a relaxed ride over challenging singletrack.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily consists of roads, many of which are charming and akin to tracks or paths. You'll ride through long stretches of 'dehesa' (wooded pastures) and past traditional farmhouses, offering a tranquil, rural experience.

faqs.question: What are the notable features or points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The trail itself is an immersive journey through serene dehesa and agricultural lands. You'll pass by the historic Herrera del Duque Town Center and the impressive Herrera del Duque Castle.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to ride this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: Spring is a beautiful time to experience the tranquil dehesa landscapes. However, be aware that in springtime, you might encounter fords that could be wet or muddy, potentially requiring you to get your feet wet.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with any other known routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, approximately 7% of this route overlaps with sections of the Camino del Levante - Etapa 3.

faqs.question: Is the Herrera del Duque - circular Puebla de Alcocer trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed on public trails in Spain, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near livestock. Be mindful of potential muddy sections in spring.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay an entrance fee for this trail, given it's in La Siberia region?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or to pay an entrance fee to ride this trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route starting and ending in Herrera del Duque. You can typically find parking within the town, particularly near the Herrera del Duque Town Center, which serves as a convenient starting point.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and rural setting, we recommend bringing plenty of water, snacks, basic bike repair tools, and appropriate clothing for the weather. In spring, consider waterproof footwear due to potential muddy fords.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or facilities near the trail?
faqs.answer: As the trail starts and ends in Herrera del Duque, you will find various cafes, restaurants, and other facilities within the town center before or after your ride.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a tranquil experience through extensive 'dehesa' landscapes and past traditional farmhouses, providing a quintessential Extremaduran rural backdrop. It's a great way to immerse yourself in the natural beauty of the region.
